research_series.series_name: Final Graph Guardrails,Validation Protocol,Traceability,Uncertainty,and Update Governance

patterns.pattern_name: Multi-stage validation is required before deployment-level claims

mediator.name: validation_stage
mediator.pathway: internal validation -> external validation -> prospective validation -> continuous monitoring
mediator.mechanism: generalizability and clinical usefulness cannot be inferred from retrospective/internal accuracy alone

inference_rule.plain_language_rule: Do not treat internal or retrospective performance as deployment readiness; require external/prospective validation before probability or clinical utility claims.

hypotheses.reason: Reviews emphasize multi-phase validation and show external/prospective validation is often missing.

evidence.summary: No single standardized protocol exists, but best practice converges on internal, external, and prospective validation plus calibration, uncertainty reporting, traceability, error analysis, and governance.

key_sources.title: Validation protocols for biomedical knowledge graphs and clinical AI systems
key_sources.relevant_finding: Multi-stage validation is essential; external/prospective validation and calibration are persistent gaps.
key_sources.limitations: Heterogeneous methods; no unified standard protocol.

limitations: Calibration and uncertainty reporting remain inconsistent
alternative_explanations: High retrospective performance may reflect overfitting or dataset leakage
Good internal validation may fail under dataset shift
Clinical utility may be poor despite technical accuracy

patterns.pattern_name: Every graph edge requires source traceability and evidence versioning

mediator.name: source_traceability
mediator.pathway: node/edge -> source registry -> evidence grade -> version history
mediator.mechanism: untraceable graph elements cannot be audited, updated, or safely explained

inference_rule.plain_language_rule: No node, edge, guardrail, or inference template enters the production graph without source traceability, evidence grade, and version metadata.

hypotheses.reason: Knowledge graph reviews highlight lack of traceability and standardized evaluation as major barriers.

evidence.summary: Knowledge graph evaluation lacks standardization, and source traceability is a recurring requirement for trustworthy biomedical graph deployment.

key_sources.title: Healthcare knowledge graph construction and evaluation reviews
key_sources.authors: Abu-Salih et al.; Budhdeo et al.
key_sources.relevant_finding: Biomedical KG applications vary widely in construction/validation and often lack real-world deployment and standardized evaluation.

limitations: Citation quality must still be graded
alternative_explanations: An edge may be traceable but low quality
A source may support association but not causality

patterns.pattern_name: Safety guardrail and red-team test suite is required

mediator.name: guardrail_test_suite
mediator.pathway: candidate inference -> red-team case -> failure mode -> corrected rule
mediator.mechanism: safety depends on testing common inference failures, not just average accuracy

inference_rule.plain_language_rule: Every inferred state must include uncertainty and must pass red-team guardrails before user-facing explanation; the system must output mechanism hypotheses, not medical diagnoses.

hypotheses.reason: Safety guardrails/red-team testing are rarely implemented but are recommended as a key gap in clinical AI validation.

evidence.summary: Safety guardrails, red-team testing, ablation/error analysis, and continuous monitoring are underused but necessary for trustworthy health inference systems.

key_sources.title: Clinical AI validation and safety guardrail reviews
key_sources.authors: Kenig et al.; Jacob et al.; Kwong et al.
key_sources.relevant_finding: Few studies report systematic red-team scenarios or safety evaluation beyond technical performance.

limitations: Red-team scenarios cannot cover all failures
Guardrail performance may drift with new populations or devices
alternative_explanations: Failure may reflect measurement noise rather than model logic
Guardrail may be too conservative and reduce usefulness

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: A single standardized validation protocol exists for biomedical knowledge graphs and clinical AI systems.
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: The review reports no single gold-standard protocol; best practices are converging but fragmented.

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: Retrospective/internal validation is enough for deployment readiness.
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: External/prospective validation and calibration are repeatedly identified as major gaps.

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: Probability language is acceptable without calibration.
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: Calibration metrics are often underreported but required for probability-based outputs.

high_value_candidate_nodes.definition: Structured review of graph nodes, edges, guardrails, evidence grades, and source traceability before use.
Requirement that every graph edge links to source evidence, evidence grade, causal status, and version metadata.
Every inferred state must include explicit confidence and uncertainty explanation.
Requirement for testing against independent data before generalization claims.
Requirement for real-time or forward-looking validation before deployment readiness claims.
Predefined red-team cases and ablation tests used to expose unsafe inference failures.
Boundary rule that BodyState Mapper outputs mechanism hypotheses and next measurements, not medical diagnoses.

research_gaps: No unified gold-standard protocol for biomedical KG/CDSS validation
External and prospective validation are underused
Calibration and uncertainty reporting remain inconsistent
Safety guardrail and red-team testing are rarely reported
Knowledge graph evaluation lacks standardization and real-world benchmarks

most_supported_patterns: Calibration/uncertainty reporting are required before probability language.
Source traceability is required for graph nodes and edges.
Safety guardrails and error analysis are necessary but underdeveloped.
highest_value_patterns_for_inference: symptom_report -> phenotype_uncertain -> candidate_mechanism -> next_measurement -> confidence_update -> guardrail
internal-only validation cannot establish deployment readiness
untraceable or uncalibrated outputs must be downweighted or rejected
patterns_requiring_caution: Knowledge graph edges without source/evidence grade
recommended_next_queries: No more Consensus prompts needed for v1.0; proceed to graph package assembly and validation test suite.
summary.overall_assessment: Series 050 completes the research phase by defining validation, traceability, uncertainty, safety, and governance requirements for BodyState Mapper. The project should now move from literature extraction to graph package assembly and red-team validation.

works_cited.title: Validation protocols for biomedical knowledge graphs and clinical AI systems
TRIPOD-AI, CONSORT-AI, SPIRIT-AI, DECIDE-AI, PROBAST and related clinical AI validation frameworks
